texto: Listen to me," Audra said. "Listen good. You don't get to touch me anymore. Not ever. You don't own me, or my children. We are not your possessions. You never really loved our children, but I do. Now, I'm going to find Sean and Louise. You can either help me or get out of my way. Which is it?

frase_original: Listen to me," Audra said. "Listen good. You don't get to touch me anymore. Not ever. You don't own me, or my children. We are not your possessions. You never really loved our children, but I do. Now, I'm going to find Sean and Louise. You can either help me or get out of my way. Which is it?

texto: Margaret took Patrick's hand in hers. "Now you two," she said, "isn't it time you gave me another grandchild? We can't have Sean growing up an only child, like Patrick."Patrick blushed and smiled as Margaret squeezed his knee. And Audra caught a glimpse of her function in the marriage, then. She shivered and counted the minutes until she could go home and retreat to the haze.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Haylen Beck: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Margaret took Patrick's hand in hers. "Now you two," she said, "isn't it time you gave me another…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, mystery, thriller pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e thriller.
